metadata.name: Global Tariff Briefing
metadata.audience: NYT colleagues tracking tariffs and trade policy globally

all.headline: Stocks Fall as Bond Rout Deepens After Jump in Oil: Markets Wrap
all.description: The selloff in global bonds deepened after another jump in oil prices fanned fears that the war in the Middle East is fueling inflation. Stocks slipped before Friday’s jobs report.

all.headline: Trump Is Roiling the Markets He Cares About the Most
all.description: Inflation and higher bond yields pose peril for Trump as midterm elections loom.

all.headline: Philippines to Shorten Work Week, Cut Power Use to Save Energy
all.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said government offices will be on a four-day work week starting on Monday in the Southeast Asian nation’s bid to save on energy as tensions in the Middle Eas

all.headline: KKR Seeks $500 Million Loan for School Operator XCL Stake Buy
all.description: KKR & Co. is seeking a $500 million loan to back its acquisition of a majority stake in school operator XCL Education Holdings Pte., according to people familiar with the matter.

all.headline: Veja os Três Assuntos que Marcaram a Semana no Brasil
all.description: Patricia Xavier traz os assuntos que foram destaque da semana. Com produção de Barbara Nascimento e edição de Raphael Almeida.  (Source: Bloomberg)

all.headline: Germany’s Solar Boom Eases Power Costs as Gas Price Jumps
all.description: Germany is seeing unusually strong solar power output in March, helping to cap electricity prices even as the conflict in the Middle East drives up the cost of energy worldwide.

all.headline: US Gasoline Pump Prices Jump to Highest Level Under Trump
all.description: US gasoline pump prices advanced to the highest level at any time under President Donald Trump, with the conflict in the Middle East disrupting energy supplies.

all.headline: Bonds Slide as War Takes ECB Hike From Fringe to Fully Priced
all.description: A week ago, anyone betting on a European Central Bank interest-rate hike was staking out a lonely, contrarian position. Now, with the Iran war threatening to stoke inflation, the trade is the consensu

all.headline: Etihad Resumes Commercial Flights as Missile Threat Persists
all.description: Etihad Airways is preparing to restart flights to around 70 destinations after almost a week of complete shutdown for one of the Middle East’s largest carriers.

all.headline: BofA’s Hartnett Sees European, Japanese Stocks at Risk From War
all.description: A prolonged Iran war is a risk to the bank-stock powered rallies in Japan and Europe as investors abandon these markets in favor of exposure to oil and the US dollar, Bank of America’s Michael Hartnet

all.headline: Five ways the Iran war could affect you - in charts
all.description: With fuel and gas prices having risen in recent days, here are some ways the conflict could affect households.

all.headline: Lenders lift mortgage rates as Iran war hits borrowing costs
all.description: Nationwide, HSBC and Coventry Building Society are all putting some mortgage rates up.

all.headline: One in 7 shops in UK has turned cashless in the past year, survey finds
all.description: Some 14% of small High Street traders have gone card only in the last year, a survey suggests.

all.headline: US eases sanctions on Russian oil sales to India during Iran conflict
all.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent gives India a 30-day waiver to buy Russian crude as a "stop gap measure".

all.headline: Anthropic vows to sue Pentagon over supply chain risk label
all.description: The supply chain risk designation of the artificial intelligence firm is a first for a US company.

all.headline: Can snacks help you sleep?
all.description: Chocolates, bars, gummies and drinks promise to help you sleep, but is the science behind them sound?

all.headline: Charity warns of impact as heating oil prices rise
all.description: The price of heating oil has risen amid the Middle East conflict, hitting many in rural areas.

all.headline: UK firms pull fixed energy deals as Iran war pushes up prices
all.description: Data suggests the number of fixed-term deals has more than halved as prices for wholesale energy surge.

all.headline: TfL hack in 2024 affected around 10 million people, BBC can reveal
all.description: TfL insists it has "kept customers informed throughout this incident and will continue to take all necessary action".

all.headline: Will petrol and diesel prices go up now?
all.description: If oil prices remain high for some time, the knock-on effects could affect the costs of fuel and food

all.headline: 'What is the game plan?': The Iran war is unsettling China and its ambitions
Trump cuts his losses on Noem after controversial tenure at homeland security
all.description: Noem was one of the administration's brightest stars when she stepped into the role, but has faced a mounting backlash in recent months.

all.headline: US and Venezuela agree to resume diplomatic ties after Maduro capture
all.description: The US said the two sides would make joint efforts to promote stability after the US seized Maduro and his wife.

all.headline: 'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
all.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

all.headline: US asked Ukraine for help fighting Iranian drones, Zelensky says
all.description: Ukraine's president says Kyiv will only help if doing so does not deplete its own air defences.

all.headline: Hungary confirms it is holding seven Ukrainian bank workers and $80m
all.description: The bank said they were in two vans carrying $80m (£60m) worth of cash between Austria and Ukraine.

all.headline: Israel strikes Beirut after evacuation warning sparks panic
all.description: Huge traffic jams form after the unprecedented warning for people to leave the Hezbollah stronghold, as Israel steps up its strikes across Lebanon.

all.headline: Savannah Guthrie plans return to NBC as search for missing mother continues
all.description: The Today programme host visited her colleagues in the office on Thursday, nearly five weeks since her mother disappeared.

all.headline: Canadian teen died from drowning after dingo attack in Australia
all.description: The body of Piper James was found in January surrounded by dingoes on the island of K'Gari.

all.headline: 2 tankers carrying India’s Reliance fuel ditch Europe, divert to Singapore, Southeast Asia
3 workers arrested over theft at Wang Fuk Court, HK$90,000 in jewellery seized
China’s securities regulator vows tougher oversight to shore up stock-market confidence
8 takeaways from the economic briefing at China’s ‘two sessions’
Sri Lanka takes control of Iranian ship, brings crew to shore
Hong Kong police arrest 3 linked to HK$10 million knifepoint robbery
China to pursue commercial health insurance to ease public strain, support drug innovation
Chinese man adopted by Canadian family reunites with birth father after being abandoned
Could Iran war confirm China’s prediction on US military’s hypersonic nightmare?
China’s last CMC general urges more ‘political rectification’ in PLA centenary countdown
Ukraine accuses Hungary of seizing cash and gold shipment, detaining bank staff
all.description: Kyiv denounces Budapest's “state terrorism and racketeering” after cash convoy and seven employees detained.

all.headline: Poland expects Iran war to delay US weapons deliveries
all.description: The U.S. and Gulf countries are fighting Iranian strikes mainly with Patriot PAC-2 and PAC-3 interceptor missiles.

all.headline: UK cops arrest 4 in Iran spying probe
all.description: Metropolitan Police say the men are suspected of scoping out "locations and individuals linked to the Jewish community" in London.

all.headline: Vier Wirtschaftsbosse gegen Merz: Krisentreffen in München
all.description: Wirtschaftswende auf dem Prüfstand: Kanzler Friedrich Merz trifft heute auf der Internationalen Handwerksmesse in München eine Art „Verbandsquartett“. Er ist zum Gespräch mit den Spitzen der Verbände

all.headline: Protecting the electricity grid is crucial for national security
OPINION: To fix Europe’s food system, start with the school lunch
all.description: Treating school meals as a logistical burden is a wasted opportunity. New data shows they are a strategic lever for the EU Green Deal and European Child Guarantee.

all.headline: The delicate art of hanging around like a lemon while your allies get lambasted
all.description: Friedrich Merz said nothing as Donald Trump launched a wave of verbal attacks — he's not the only one to have sat in silence.

all.headline: Can Donald Trump save Viktor Orbán?
all.description: As Hungary’s prime minister trails in the polls, MAGA heavyweights mobilize to shore up their most loyal European ally.

all.headline: ‘The catastrophe is coming’: The battle to fix Britain’s crumbling parliament
all.description: British MPs worry that voters will be 'furious' with a revamp of parliament — but the safety warnings keep piling up.

all.headline: Inside the fight to fix Parliament
all.description: The Palace of Westminster towers over the River Thames as a symbol of British democracy. But look a little closer and the building is falling apart. From fire risks and asbestos to crumbling stonework
